journal_entry.rationale: Utility expense is recognized in the period incurred under accrual accounting (IFRS). The debit to Utilities Expense (c=1, expense increase) records the cost of utilities consumed. The credit to Accounts Payable or Accrued Liabilities (a=1, liability increase) recognizes the obligation to pay the utility provider. No tax is applicable as this is an accrual entry; tax would have been included in the $50 bill amount if applicable.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense that has been incurred but not yet paid. The debit side records utility expense increase (1 expense line). The credit side (not classified here) would increase accounts payable/accrued liabilities.

debit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense that has been incurred but not yet paid. The debit side records utility expense increase (1 expense line). The credit side (not classified here) would increase accounts payable/accrued liabilities.

output.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now even though it hasn't been paid yet. The credit side records the liability increase (accounts payable/accrued expenses) for the amount owed to the utility company.

credit_classifier.reason: Accruing a utility bill means recognizing the expense now even though it hasn't been paid yet. The credit side records the liability increase (accounts payable/accrued expenses) for the amount owed to the utility company.
